questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a parasitic plant?
questions.explanation: Cuscuta, also known as Amarbel, is a parasitic plant that lacks chlorophyll and obtains its nutrition by growing on other plants (host plants) and drawing nutrients from them. Mushrooms are saprophytes, green algae are autotrophs, and Venus Flytrap is an insectivorous plant.

questions.question: The process of taking food into the body is called:
questions.explanation: Ingestion is the process of taking food into the body. Digestion is the breakdown of complex food substances into simpler ones. Absorption is the process by which digested food passes into the blood vessels. Egestion is the removal of undigested food from the body.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and metals are generally good conductors of heat, meaning they allow heat to pass through them easily. Wood, plastic, and air are insulators, meaning they do not conduct heat well.

questions.question: A substance that turns blue litmus red is generally a/an:
questions.explanation: Acids are substances that turn blue litmus paper red. Bases turn red litmus paper blue. Salts are formed from the reaction of an acid and a base and may be neutral, acidic, or basic depending on the strength of the acid and base. Neutral substances do not change the colour of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Burning of wood is a chemical change because new substances (ash, smoke, carbon dioxide) are formed, and the change is irreversible. Melting of ice, boiling of water, and tearing of paper are physical changes as no new substances are formed, and the chemical composition remains the same.

questions.question: The process of breakdown of food in the cell with the release of energy is called:
questions.explanation: Respiration is the process by which organisms break down glucose (food) in their cells to release energy. Photosynthesis is the process by which plants make their own food. Digestion is the breakdown of food in the digestive system. Transpiration is the loss of water vapor from plants.

questions.question: Which component of blood is responsible for clotting?
questions.explanation: Platelets are tiny blood cells that help your body form clots to stop bleeding. Red blood cells carry oxygen, white blood cells fight infection, and plasma is the liquid component of blood.

questions.question: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a flower is called:
questions.explanation: Pollination is the process of transferring pollen grains from the anther (male part) to the stigma (female part) of a flower. Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes. Germination is the process by which a seed sprouts. Dispersal is the spreading of seeds or fruits away from the parent plant.

questions.question: If a car travels a distance of 100 km in 2 hours, what is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Speed is calculated by dividing the distance traveled by the time taken. So, Speed = Distance / Time = 100 km / 2 hours = 50 km/h.

questions.question: Which of the following is a safety device used in electrical circuits?
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device that protects electrical circuits from excessive current. If the current exceeds a safe limit, the fuse wire melts and breaks the circuit, preventing damage to appliances and potential hazards. A switch controls the flow of current, a battery provides power, and a bulb is a load.

questions.question: When an acid reacts with a base, it forms:
questions.explanation: The reaction between an acid and a base is called a neutralization reaction. In this reaction, an acid and a base react to form a salt and water. For example, hydrochloric acid (acid) reacts with sodium hydroxide (base) to form sodium chloride (salt) and water.

questions.question: During exhalation, the diaphragm moves:
questions.explanation: During exhalation, the diaphragm relaxes and moves upwards, pushing air out of the lungs. During inhalation, the diaphragm contracts and moves downwards, allowing air to enter the lungs.

questions.question: The blood vessels that carry blood from the heart to all parts of the body are called:
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from the heart to various parts of the body (except pulmonary artery). Veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins, facilitating the exchange of substances.

questions.question: Which of the following is a safety device used in electrical circuits to prevent damage due to excessive current?
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device that melts and breaks the circuit when the current flowing through it exceeds a safe limit, thus protecting electrical appliances and wiring from damage. A switch is used to turn a circuit on or off. A bulb produces light. A battery provides electrical energy.

questions.question: A substance that turns blue litmus paper red is generally a/an:
questions.explanation: Acids are substances that turn blue litmus paper red. Bases turn red litmus paper blue. Salts and neutral substances generally do not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice is a physical change because only the state of water changes from solid to liquid, but its chemical composition remains the same (H2O). Burning of wood, rusting of iron, and digestion of food are all chemical changes, as new substances are formed.

questions.question: The tiny pores on the surface of leaves through which gaseous exchange takes place are called:
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores present on the surface of leaves, primarily on the underside, through which plants exchange gases like carbon dioxide and oxygen with the atmosphere. Xylem and phloem are involved in transportation, and chloroplasts are sites of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which component of blood helps in clotting?
questions.explanation: Platelets are tiny cell fragments in the blood that play a crucial role in blood clotting. When a blood vessel is injured, platelets rush to the site and form a plug, helping to stop the bleeding. Red blood cells carry oxygen, white blood cells fight infection, and plasma is the liquid component of blood.

questions.question: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma is called:
questions.explanation: Pollination is the process of transferring pollen grains from the anther (male reproductive part) to the stigma (female reproductive part) of a flower. Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes. Germination is the process by which a seed sprouts. Dispersal is the spreading of seeds or fruits.

questions.question: Which blood component helps in the clotting of blood?
questions.explanation: Platelets are tiny cell fragments in the blood that play a crucial role in blood clotting. When a blood vessel is injured, platelets rush to the site and form a plug, initiating the clotting process to stop bleeding.

questions.question: Which of the following is a safety device used in electric circuits?
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device that protects electrical circuits from excessive current. If the current exceeds a safe limit, the fuse wire melts and breaks the circuit, preventing damage to appliances and potential hazards. A switch controls the flow of current, a battery provides power, and a wire conducts electricity.

questions.question: Which of these substances would turn blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Baking soda solution and soap solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and would not change the colour of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Burning of wood is a chemical change because new substances (ash, smoke, carbon dioxide) are formed, and the change is generally irreversible. Melting of ice, boiling of water, and tearing of paper are physical changes as no new substances are formed, and the chemical composition remains the same.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-renewable source of energy?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable source of energy because it takes millions of years to form and its reserves are finite. Solar energy, wind energy, and hydropower are renewable sources of energy as they can be replenished naturally over a relatively short period.

questions.question: Which part of a flower develops into a fruit after fertilization?
questions.explanation: After fertilization, the ovary of a flower develops into the fruit, and the ovules inside the ovary develop into seeds. The stamen is the male reproductive part, petals attract pollinators, and sepals protect the bud.

questions.question: Which of the following components is essential for an electric circuit to be complete and allow current to flow?
questions.explanation: For an electric circuit to be complete and allow current to flow, it needs a power source (like a cell/battery), connecting wires, a load (like a bulb), and often a switch to control the flow of current. All these components are essential for a functional circuit.

questions.question: Which of these materials is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and metals are generally good conductors of heat, meaning heat can easily pass through them. Wood, plastic, and air are poor conductors of heat, also known as insulators.

questions.question: Which of the following substances turns blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Baking soda solution and soap solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and would not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice is a physical change because only the state of water changes from solid to liquid, but its chemical composition remains the same. Burning of wood, rusting of iron, and digestion of food are chemical changes as new substances are formed.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, the muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is the end product of this respiration?
questions.explanation: When muscle cells respire anaerobically (without oxygen) during heavy exercise, they produce lactic acid. This accumulation of lactic acid can cause muscle cramps. Aerobic respiration produces carbon dioxide and water. Ethanol and carbon dioxide are products of anaerobic respiration in yeast.

questions.question: Which part of a flower contains the pollen grains?
questions.explanation: The anther is part of the stamen (male reproductive organ) of a flower and produces pollen grains, which contain the male gametes. Sepals protect the bud, petals attract pollinators, and the ovary contains ovules.

questions.question: A car travels a distance of 100 km in 2 hours. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Speed is calculated by dividing the distance traveled by the time taken. In this case, Speed = Distance / Time = 100 km / 2 hours = 50 km/h.

questions.question: Which of the following devices works on the heating effect of electric current?
questions.explanation: An electric heater works on the principle that when electric current passes through a high-resistance wire, it generates heat. Electric bells and motors work on the magnetic effect of electric current, and electric fans use a motor to rotate blades.

questions.question: Where does the complete digestion of food primarily occur in humans?
questions.explanation: The small intestine is the primary site for the complete digestion and absorption of food in humans. While digestion begins in the mouth and continues in the stomach, most of the chemical digestion and nutrient absorption takes place in the small intestine with the help of digestive juices from the liver and pancreas.

questions.question: Which of these is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and metals are generally good conductors of heat, meaning they allow heat to pass through them easily. Wood, plastic, and air are insulators, which means they do not conduct heat well.

questions.question: Which substance is used as an indicator to test for the presence of an acid or a base?
questions.explanation: Litmus paper is a common indicator used to test whether a solution is acidic or basic. Blue litmus paper turns red in acidic solutions, and red litmus paper turns blue in basic solutions. Salt, sugar, and water are not indicators.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is the end product of this anaerobic respiration?
questions.explanation: When muscle cells respire anaerobically (without oxygen) during heavy exercise, they produce lactic acid. This accumulation of lactic acid can cause muscle cramps. Aerobic respiration produces carbon dioxide and water, while anaerobic respiration in yeast produces ethanol and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: When a blue litmus paper is dipped into a solution of baking soda, what change will be observed?
questions.explanation: Baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) is a basic substance. Bases turn red litmus paper blue, but they do not change the color of blue litmus paper. Therefore, when blue litmus paper is dipped into a baking soda solution, it will remain blue.

questions.question: Which of the following processes involves the absorption of heat from the surroundings?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice is an endothermic process, meaning it absorbs heat from its surroundings to change from a solid to a liquid state. Burning of wood (combustion) releases heat. Condensation of water vapour and freezing of water are exothermic processes, meaning they release heat to the surroundings.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a physical change?
options: The chemical composition of the substance changes.
Changes in state, shape, or size can occur.
questions.explanation: A physical change involves a change in the physical properties of a substance, such as its state, shape, or size, but its chemical composition remains the same. No new substance is formed, and these changes are often reversible. For example, melting ice into water is a physical change because water (H2O) is still water, just in a different state. The option 'The chemical composition of the substance changes' describes a chemical change, not a physical change.

questions.question: The SI unit of speed is:
questions.explanation: The SI (International System of Units) unit for speed is meter per second (m/s). While other units like km/h are commonly used, m/s is the standard unit in scientific contexts.

questions.question: Which of the following is a device that converts electrical energy into light energy?
questions.explanation: An electric bulb converts electrical energy primarily into light energy (and some heat). An electric motor converts electrical energy into mechanical energy. An electric bell converts electrical energy into sound energy. An electric heater converts electrical energy into heat energy.

questions.question: A substance that turns red litmus paper blue is a/an:
questions.explanation: Bases are substances that turn red litmus paper blue. Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Salts and neutral substances generally do not change the colour of litmus paper.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, the muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is the byproduct of this process?
questions.explanation: When muscle cells respire anaerobically (without oxygen) during heavy exercise, they produce lactic acid. This accumulation of lactic acid can cause muscle cramps. Carbon dioxide and water are products of aerobic respiration, and alcohol is produced during anaerobic respiration in yeast.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is the end product of this process?
questions.explanation: When muscle cells respire anaerobically (without oxygen) during heavy exercise, glucose is partially broken down to produce lactic acid. This accumulation of lactic acid can cause muscle cramps. Carbon dioxide and water are products of aerobic respiration. Ethanol and carbon dioxide are products of anaerobic respiration in yeast.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-biodegradable waste?
questions.explanation: A plastic bottle is a non-biodegradable waste, meaning it cannot be broken down by natural processes and microorganisms. Vegetable peels, paper, and cotton cloth are biodegradable and can decompose over time.

questions.question: The tiny pores on the surface of leaves through which gaseous exchange occurs are called:
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores present on the surface of leaves, primarily on the underside, that allow for the exchange of gases like carbon dioxide and oxygen during photosynthesis and respiration. Xylem and phloem are vascular tissues for transport, and chloroplasts are organelles where photosynthesis takes place.

questions.question: Which blood vessels carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body?
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood (except pulmonary artery) away from the heart to various parts of the body. Veins carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins, facilitating exchange of substances. Lymph vessels are part of the lymphatic system.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of wastewater?
options: Water used for irrigation in fields
questions.explanation: Wastewater is any water that has been affected by human use. Sewage from homes and industries contains human waste, chemicals, and other pollutants, making it a primary component of wastewater. Clean drinking water, rainwater, and irrigation water are generally not considered wastewater unless they become contaminated.

questions.question: Which of the following substances would turn blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Baking soda solution and soap solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and would not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of ice is a physical change because only the state of water changes from solid to liquid, but its chemical composition remains the same (H2O). Burning of wood, rusting of iron, and digestion of food are all chemical changes as new substances are formed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a safety device used in electrical circuits to prevent damage from excessive current?
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device that contains a thin wire designed to melt and break the circuit if the current flowing through it exceeds a safe limit. This prevents damage to appliances and wiring due to short circuits or overloads. A switch controls the flow of current, a battery provides power, and a bulb converts electrical energy into light.

questions.question: Which type of thermometer is used to measure the temperature of the human body?
questions.explanation: A clinical thermometer is specifically designed to measure the temperature of the human body. It has a kink that prevents the mercury level from falling on its own, allowing time to read the temperature. While digital thermometers are also used, the clinical thermometer is the traditional mercury-based one for this purpose.

questions.question: Which of the following substances will turn blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Baking soda solution, soap solution, and lime water are all basic (alkaline) and would turn red litmus blue or have no effect on blue litmus.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, we sometimes get muscle cramps. This is due to the accumulation of:
questions.explanation: During heavy exercise, when there is insufficient oxygen supply to the muscle cells, anaerobic respiration takes place. This process produces lactic acid, which accumulates in the muscles and causes cramps.

questions.question: The blood vessel that carries oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body is the:
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood (except pulmonary artery) from the heart to various parts of the body. Veins carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins. Vena cava is a large vein that carries deoxygenated blood to the heart.

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper wire is a good conductor of electricity, meaning it allows electric current to flow through it easily. Rubber, plastic, and wood are all insulators, which resist the flow of electric current.

questions.question: Which of these substances turns blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Baking soda solution and soap solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and would not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which mode of heat transfer does not require a medium?
questions.explanation: Radiation is the mode of heat transfer that does not require any material medium. Heat from the sun reaches us by radiation. Conduction requires direct contact, and convection involves the movement of fluids (liquids or gases).

questions.question: A substance that turns blue litmus paper red is a/an:
questions.explanation: Acids are substances that turn blue litmus paper red. Bases turn red litmus paper blue. Salts are formed from the reaction of an acid and a base and do not change the colour of litmus paper significantly. Neutral substances also do not change the colour of litmus paper.

questions.question: The tiny pores on the surface of leaves through which exchange of gases takes place are called:
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores present on the surface of leaves, primarily on the underside, through which exchange of gases (carbon dioxide and oxygen) and transpiration (loss of water vapour) occur. Xylem and phloem are vascular tissues, and roots absorb water and minerals.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for transporting water and minerals from the roots to the leaves in plants?
questions.explanation: Xylem is the plant tissue responsible for transporting water and dissolved minerals from the roots upwards to the rest of the plant, including the leaves. Phloem transports food (sugars) from the leaves to other parts. Stomata are pores for gas exchange, and chlorophyll is the pigment for photosynthesis.

questions.question: The process by which plants produce new plants from their vegetative parts like stem, root, or leaf is called:
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction in plants involves the production of new plants from vegetative parts like stem, root, or leaf, without the involvement of seeds or spores. Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of male and female gametes. Pollination is the transfer of pollen, and fertilization is the fusion of gametes.

questions.question: Which blood component helps in clotting of blood?
questions.explanation: Platelets are tiny cell fragments in the blood that play a crucial role in blood clotting. When a blood vessel is injured, platelets rush to the site and form a plug, initiating the clotting process to stop bleeding.

questions.question: Which of these substances will turn blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic. Soap solution and baking soda solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and has no effect on litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to make an electromagnet?
options: An iron nail and a coil of wire
questions.explanation: An electromagnet is created by winding a coil of insulated wire around a soft iron core (like an iron nail) and passing an electric current through the coil. The iron nail becomes magnetized only when the current flows.

questions.question: Which part of the digestive system absorbs most of the digested food?
questions.explanation: The small intestine is the primary site for the absorption of digested food. Its inner surface has millions of tiny finger-like projections called villi, which increase the surface area for efficient absorption of nutrients into the bloodstream. The stomach primarily digests proteins, the large intestine absorbs water, and the oesophagus transports food.

questions.question: What is the normal temperature of the human body in degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: The normal temperature of the human body is approximately 37°C. While 98.6°F is also a normal human body temperature, the question specifically asks for degrees Celsius. 0°C is the freezing point of water, and 100°C is the boiling point of water.

questions.question: Which of the following substances is acidic in nature?
questions.explanation: Lemon juice contains citric acid, which makes it acidic. Baking soda solution, soap solution, and lime water are all basic (alkaline) in nature. You can test this using litmus paper; lemon juice would turn blue litmus red.

questions.question: A car travels a distance of 120 km in 2 hours. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Speed is calculated by dividing the distance traveled by the time taken. In this case, Speed = Distance / Time = 120 km / 2 hours = 60 km/h.

questions.question: Which part of the flower develops into a fruit after fertilization?
questions.explanation: After fertilization, the ovary of the flower develops into the fruit, and the ovules inside the ovary develop into seeds. The stamen is the male reproductive part, petals are for attraction, and sepals protect the bud.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is the best conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and metals are generally excellent conductors of heat, meaning they allow heat to pass through them easily. Wood, plastic, and air are poor conductors of heat (insulators).

questions.question: Which of the following substances will turn blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Soap solution and baking soda solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Salt solution is generally neutral and would not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and a good conductor of heat, meaning it allows heat to pass through it easily. Wood, plastic, and air are insulators, which resist the flow of heat.

questions.question: Which substance turns blue litmus paper red?
questions.explanation: Acids are substances that turn blue litmus paper red. Bases turn red litmus paper blue. Neutral solutions and salts generally do not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Burning of wood is a chemical change because new substances (ash, smoke, gases) are formed, and the change is irreversible. Melting of ice, boiling of water, and tearing of paper are physical changes as no new substances are formed.

questions.question: The blood vessels that carry blood away from the heart to different parts of the body are called:
questions.explanation: Arteries are the blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from the heart to various parts of the body. Veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart, and capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a saprotrophic mode of nutrition?
options: Green plants making their own food
A mushroom growing on decaying wood
A cow grazing in a field
questions.explanation: Saprotrophic nutrition is a mode of nutrition where organisms obtain nutrients from dead and decaying organic matter. Mushrooms are fungi that exhibit this mode of nutrition by secreting digestive juices onto the dead matter and then absorbing the digested nutrients. Green plants show autotrophic nutrition, lions show holozoic (carnivorous) nutrition, and cows show holozoic (herbivorous) nutrition.

questions.question: Which of the following is a reversible change?
questions.explanation: A reversible change is a change that can be undone, meaning the original substance can be recovered. Melting of ice is a reversible change because water (liquid) can be frozen back into ice (solid). Burning of wood, rusting of iron, and cooking an egg are all irreversible changes as new substances are formed and cannot be easily converted back to their original forms.

questions.question: Which blood vessels carry blood away from the heart to different parts of the body?
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from the heart to various parts of the body (except the pulmonary artery which carries deoxygenated blood to the lungs). Veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels where exchange of substances occurs.

questions.question: The process of fusion of male and female gametes is called:
questions.explanation: Fertilization is the process where the male gamete (pollen) fuses with the female gamete (ovule) to form a zygote, which eventually develops into a seed. Pollination is the transfer of pollen. Germination is the process by which a seed sprouts. Dispersal is the spreading of seeds.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, the muscles may respire anaerobically. What is produced during this process?
questions.explanation: When there is insufficient oxygen during heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically, producing lactic acid and a small amount of energy. This lactic acid accumulation can cause muscle cramps. Carbon dioxide and water are products of aerobic respiration. Alcohol and carbon dioxide are products of anaerobic respiration in yeast.

questions.question: The blood vessel that carries oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body is called:
questions.explanation: Arteries carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to various parts of the body. Veins carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins, facilitating the exchange of substances. Vena cava is a large vein that carries deoxygenated blood to the heart.

questions.question: Which of the following is a renewable resource found in forests?
questions.explanation: Timber (wood) is a renewable resource because trees can be replanted and grown again. Minerals and fossil fuels are non-renewable resources, and plastic is a man-made material, not a natural forest resource.

questions.question: Which of the following substances would turn blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus red. Lemon juice is acidic due to the presence of citric acid. Soap solution and baking soda solution are basic and would turn red litmus blue. Distilled water is neutral and would not change the color of litmus paper.

questions.question: During exhalation, the ribs move:
questions.explanation: During exhalation, the diaphragm relaxes and moves upwards, and the ribs move downwards and inwards. This decreases the volume of the chest cavity, pushing air out of the lungs.

questions.question: A substance that turns red litmus paper blue is generally a/an:
questions.explanation: Bases are substances that turn red litmus paper blue. Acids turn blue litmus paper red. Neutral substances do not change the colour of either red or blue litmus paper. Salts are formed from the reaction of an acid and a base.

questions.question: The process of taking in oxygen-rich air into the body is called:
questions.explanation: Inhalation is the process of taking in oxygen-rich air into the body. Exhalation is the process of giving out carbon dioxide-rich air. Breathing is the overall process of inhalation and exhalation. Respiration is the process of breaking down food to release energy, which involves breathing.

questions.question: Which component of blood is responsible for transporting oxygen to different parts of the body?
questions.explanation: Red Blood Cells (RBCs) contain a pigment called haemoglobin, which binds with oxygen and transports it to all parts of the body. Plasma is the liquid part of blood, white blood cells fight infections, and platelets help in blood clotting.

questions.question: During heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is produced during this process?
questions.explanation: When there is insufficient oxygen during heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically, producing lactic acid and a small amount of energy. questions.question: Which of these is the best conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and metals are generally good conductors of heat, meaning they allow heat to pass through them easily. Wood, plastic, and air are insulators, which resist the flow of heat.

questions.question: Which part of the plant absorbs water and minerals from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots are responsible for anchoring the plant and absorbing water and minerals from the soil. Leaves are primarily involved in photosynthesis, stems provide support and transport, and flowers are for reproduction.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to make the filament of an electric bulb?
questions.explanation: Tungsten is used to make the filament of an electric bulb because it has a very high melting point and can withstand the high temperatures required to glow brightly without melting. Copper, aluminium, and iron have lower melting points and are not suitable for this purpose.

questions.question: The blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body are called:
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood (except pulmonary artery) away from the heart to various parts of the body. Veins carry carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins.

questions.question: The vessels that carry blood from the heart to all parts of the body are called:
questions.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from the heart to various parts of the body (except the pulmonary artery which carries deoxygenated blood to the lungs). Veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart. Capillaries are tiny blood vessels that connect arteries and veins.

questions.question: Which of the following is a safety device used in electric circuits to prevent damage due to excessive current?

This prevents damage to appliances and wiring from overheating. A switch controls the flow of current, a battery provides power, and a bulb converts electrical energy into light.

questions.question: A substance that turns blue litmus red is generally:

questions.question: During heavy exercise, the muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is produced during this process?

questions.question: Which part of the heart pumps oxygen-rich blood to the rest of the body?

questions.question: Which part of the digestive system is primarily responsible for the absorption of digested food?

questions.question: During heavy exercise, muscle cells respire anaerobically. What is the end product of this anaerobic respiration in muscle cells?

questions.question: Which component of blood is responsible for clotting and stopping bleeding?
